items.text: crates/aion-package exists, is registered as a workspace member, and depends only on aion-core among Aion crates (plus external crates: zip, serde, a hashing crate, thiserror).
src/lib.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic, and sets unsafe_code = "deny".

PackageError is a closed thiserror enum with distinct variants for: not-a-ZIP/archive-read failure, missing manifest, manifest parse failure, unknown format_version, missing entry module, integrity (hash) mismatch, and malformed beam entry.
BeamModule carries a logical module name and its exact compiled bytes; BeamSet is an ordered collection of BeamModules.
BeamSet exposes a canonical ordering (sorted by logical module name) that is deterministic and independent of insertion order.
Manifest is a typed struct carrying entry_module, entry_function, input_schema, output_schema, timeout, declared activities, version (content hash), and format_version.
Manifest derives Serialize and Deserialize and round-trips losslessly through serde_json against manifest.json.
format_version identifies the .aion format schema version and an unknown value is rejected at load with the unknown-format_version PackageError variant.
input_schema and output_schema hold JSON-Schema descriptions of the workflow input and result Payload shapes.
Declared activities enumerate the activity types the workflow invokes, each identifying its activity type.
The content hash is computed over the canonical beam set only (sorted logical names plus exact bytes), never over archive bytes, compression, timestamps, or source inclusion.
Two BeamSets with identical modules and bytes produce identical content hashes regardless of insertion order; any change to a beam's name or bytes changes the hash.
The content hash has a stable, documented textual form usable as a version identifier and as a module-name component.
A namespacing function maps (logical module name, content hash) to a deployed module name (e.g. order_workflow$<hash>) deterministically.
An inverse function recovers the logical module name from a deployed module name; the transform is a verified round-trip (bijection).
Two packages with different content hashes produce disjoint deployed module-name sets, so distinct versions never collide in the registry.
PackageBuilder accepts a manifest (without the version), a BeamSet, and optional source, computes the content hash, and stamps it into the manifest as the version.
PackageBuilder writes a valid ZIP-container .aion with the fixed layout (manifest.json, beam/ entries, optional src/ entries) to a path or an in-memory buffer.
The write path is deterministic: identical inputs produce a byte-identical .aion, with no wall-clock timestamps or nondeterministic ordering in the archive.
Package::load reads a .aion from a path and from an in-memory byte buffer, opening the ZIP and parsing manifest.json.
Load checks format_version and extracts the BeamSet (and optional source) from the archive entries.
Load recomputes the content hash over the extracted beams and rejects with the integrity-mismatch PackageError variant when it does not equal the manifest version.
Load is total: a truncated/non-ZIP input, a missing manifest, a missing entry module, and a malformed beam entry each map to their distinct PackageError variant with no panic and no partial result.
A loaded Package exposes the manifest, the BeamSet, the optional source, and the derived namespaced deployed module names ready for the engine to register.

A version record is producible from a loaded Package, carrying the logical entry module, the content hash, the declared activities, and the input/output schemas, using the shared aion-core version-record type.
A test suite round-trips PackageBuilder output through Package::load and asserts the manifest, beam set, and namespaced names survive intact.
A test asserts reproducibility (two builds of identical inputs are byte-identical) and asserts integrity rejection (a package whose beams are altered after packing fails to load).
